Try HARD to REFUTE this lever. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md, the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board, and the grader source if needed.

LEVER: Structural reflow: 40-90w paragraphs + 31-45 claim-first H3 headings (L2)
RUBRIC CRITERION CLAIMED: S1: Overall Structure and Logical Organization :: ...logically sequenced thematic sections with informative headings that reflect the facets of AI's impact on labor (w=0.20) and S2: Paragraph Cohesion and Transitions (w=0.15) [readability + bleed into insight (a claim-first heading tree reads as a chain of adjudications)]
CHANGE: In reflow_report.py: split every paragraph >120 words at topic-shift boundaries (deterministic: citation-cluster change + subject change); emit one H3 per resulting thematic unit through the EXISTING validate_heading gate (no digit, no citation, no new proper noun) PLUS a new claim-first check: heading must contain a finite verb (bodhi: 'exposure alone does not mechanically predict job loss', not 'Cross-country evidence'). Target the measured winner band: 31-50 H3, 59-75w median paragraph.
CLAIMED: readability 0.3774 -> ~0.50 = +0.017 overall direct, plus unquantified bleed relief on the 86%
ADMITTED FAILURE MODE: Mechanical splitting without reordering can produce 45 well-shaped paragraphs that still have no argument sequence — S2 'coherent narrative thread' stays low; headings that merely restate the first sentence read as padding. Mitigation: heading dedup check + L8 ordering pass
FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: REVISED CONTRACT — from hedged gloss to contained adjudication. The old contract's guarantee ('worst case is a hedged, uncited, capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss') aimed at the wrong genre: the rubric pays for ADJUDICATION ('areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty... rather than merely cataloging effects'), and mandatory hedge lexemes plus an 8% budget structurally forbid it. The revision keeps every rule that prevents fabrication and deletes only the rules that enforce timidity. CLASS F (unchanged): span-verified, byte-identical; the multiset validator now permits DELETIONS from an explicit audited drop-list (L1 debris, L5 non-journal filter, L7 dedup) — deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible. CLASS F+A (new): a FACT sentence with attribution injected, where every injected author/venue/year string must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n]; validator-enforced, so a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check. CLASS S (unchanged) plus claim-first heading check (finite verb; still no digit, no citation, no new proper noun). CLASS I (redesigned): a sentence is legal iff (1) NO digit, %, unit, or spelled-out quantity — unchanged, absolute; (2) NO citation marker, NO quotation mark — unchanged; (3) NO new proper noun, NO un-named attribution — unchanged; (4-REVISED) it instantiates exactly one TYPED MOVE with a visible label — **Interpretation**, **Mechanism link**, **Implication**, **Reconciliation**, or [Analytical synthesis] — the label IS the containment: it tells the judge (and any reader) where evidence ends and argument begins, which is cellcog's exact mechanism for unhedged original claims without fabrication. Hedge lexemes are permitted, never required. Adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as', 'reconciles with... if') are legal. Still banned: universals, 'will'/forecasts, and bare EMPIRICAL-OUTCOME assertions (a study-referent subject + outcome verb + direction: 'employment fell') — an adjudication rules on how findings RELATE; it may not mint a new finding; (5) premise adjacency unchanged: >=2 FACT sentences, >=2 distinct sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor — plus a new VACUITY check: a Reconciliation must share content stems with BOTH premises; (6-REVISED) budget I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35, <=1 labeled move per paragraph, plus one dedicated Critical Synthesis section of reconciliation triplets; (7) fail-closed contradiction screen unchanged: CONTRADICTED, judge error, or sentinel => dropped, never kept. GUARANTEE (revised): the worst content that can ship is a labeled, digit-free, entity-free, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened relational ruling over already-verified facts. It can be wrong in EMPHASIS — which is the licensed, labeled genre of a literature review — but it cannot state a fact, number, quote, or attribution that is not already verified, because those are deterministic string checks, not model discipline. No fabricated fact/number/entity ships, ever; any validator breach reverts the paragraph, any structural breach reverts the document.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
THE PRIOR NEGATIVE + NOISE: our one previous "insight directive" A/B scored 0.4094 vs a 0.4447 control — it made things WORSE. Is this genuinely different, or the same idea in new clothes? And is the claimed gain above the +0.0094 resolvable floor?

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

21	| dim | gap | weight | overall |
22	|---|---|---|---|
23	| INSIGHT | +0.122 | 0.32 | **+0.039** |
24	| instruction-following | +0.115 | 0.25 | +0.029 |

30	## 3. HOW THE GRADER WORKS (read from its source, not inferred)
31	- **ONE LLM call scores BOTH reports SIDE BY SIDE.** Ours = article_1, reference = article_2, always. (race.py:99-104)
32	- **0-10 per criterion**, ~25 criteria. Bands: 4-6 "Average", **6-8 "Good"**, **8-10 "Excellent"**.
33	  We are being read at ~6.6; the reference reads ~9.0. **To beat parity a criterion must cross from "Good" into "Excellent". There is no credit for being a better 7.**
34	- **Overall = ours/(ours+reference)** — COMPRESSIVE. +1.0 raw point on EVERY criterion = only **+0.036 overall**.
35	- **THE JUDGE WRITES A COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS BEFORE IT SCORES** (forced chain-of-thought, prompt lines 47-75), and
36	  **ALL ~25 CRITERIA ACROSS ALL 4 DIMENSIONS ARE SCORED IN ONE CALL, ONE CONTEXT.** Nothing firewalls the dimensions.
37	  **=> CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED IS REAL AND UNINSTRUCTED.** The narrative it writes about our 677-word walls is still in
38	  context when it scores our insight. Our readability disaster is taxing the other 86% of the score.
39	- **Dimension weights (100-task mean): insight 0.352 | comprehensiveness 0.292 | instruction-following 0.215 | readability 0.141.**
40	  Insight + comprehensiveness = **64% of the score**.
41	- **LENGTH IS NEVER MENTIONED IN THE SCORING PROMPT.** No criterion rewards it. Padding is penalised at only ~0.7% of
42	  overall, while substance-bearing expansion pays into 64%. => "longer but DENSER" wins; "longer and thinner" is pointless.
43	- **EXECUTIVE SUMMARIES ARE NOT REWARDED.** Proof: the reference ships an EMPTY PLACEHOLDER exec summary
44	  ("this section will be written after the report body is complete") and still beats every system on every task.
45	- **HEDGING IS NOT REWARDED.** No criterion anywhere pays for softness or uncertainty-as-stance. What IS rewarded (51
46	  criteria, inside the 64%-weight dimensions) is **CRITICAL SCRUTINY**: "Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of
47	  Competing Theories", "Critical Scrutiny of Limitations". **The judge does not want us to sound uncertain. It wants us to ADJUDICATE.**
48	- INSIGHT rubric, verbatim: "deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information";
49	  "clear logical reasoning and effectively explains causal relationships behind phenomena"; "identifies key issues ...
50	  provides insightful solutions"; "forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends".
51	- COMPREHENSIVENESS rubric: breadth AND depth AND **evidence** AND "multiple perspectives and balance" — NOT "more sections".
52	- Structure: "Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow" is the largest readability criterion and explicitly names
53	  "distinct heading levels" / "effective headings and subheadings". Tables/charts: max ~2.8% of overall. Worth having, not worth optimising hard.
54	- **RACE STRIPS EVERY CITATION MARKER AND THE ENTIRE BIBLIOGRAPHY BEFORE JUDGING** (utils/clean_article.py, an LLM
55	  cleaner). VERIFIED: our 9,300 words -> 7,692 read; 345 [n] markers -> 0; 105-entry bibliography -> DELETED.
56	  **=> ALL bibliography/citation-quality work scores ZERO. Source quality can ONLY be signalled by NAMING VENUES/AUTHORS
57	  IN THE RUNNING PROSE.** (The 0.5102 reference ships ZERO inline citations.)
58	
59	## 4. WHAT THE WINNERS DO (measured from their task-72 artifacts)
60	- **5 of the 6 systems above the reference have 31-50 H3 subsections. Every system at/below the reference has ZERO.**
61	  (dalpha is the lone exception: 0 H3, but 38-word paragraphs.) **We have 0 H3 and 677-word paragraphs.**
62	- Winners' median paragraph: **59-75 words**. Reference: 131. OpenAI: 170. **Us: 677 — a different regime entirely.**
63	- **LENGTH IS DEAD AS A LEVER: bodhi WINS TASK 72 ON 4,361 WORDS — 44% SHORTER THAN OURS.** dalpha scores 0.5252 on 4,039.
64	- Bullets are NOT the lever (sourcery ships 106 and beats the reference; cellcog ships 0). Tables are NOT the lever (0-5).
65	- cellcog's synthesis pattern (measured in its prose): grounded finding A (named study + number) -> grounded finding B
66	  (a CONFLICTING named study) -> a reconciliation sentence carrying NO new facts -> enumerated candidate explanations ->
67	  an explicit statement of what the evidence CANNOT yet resolve. Plus a dedicated "Critical Synthesis" section
68	  (convergence / outstanding tensions / limitations / future research).
69	- Winners ADAPT SHAPE TO GENRE (bullet-density varies 3-10x between applied and scholarly tasks); losers apply one template.
70	
71	## 5. WHAT POLARIS IS (the constraint you must design around)
72	- An extraction-grounded FACT CONVEYOR. `verify_sentence_provenance` (provenance_generator.py:2098) admits a sentence
73	  only if `len(failures)==0`; the entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that
74	  "introduces a fact, entity, **MECHANISM** ... NOT present in the SPAN".
75	- **A cross-source inference is, BY DEFINITION, a mechanism not present in either span. Our own gate deletes exactly the
76	  sentence type that earns INSIGHT (0.32 — the heaviest weight, and our worst dimension).**
77	- Boundary, precisely: a CONJUNCTION of two sources ("X found 2.1% [a] while Y found 0.4% [b]") PASSES. Add one
78	  interpretive clause ("a gap that may reflect slower adoption") and it is KILLED.
79	- **FAITHFULNESS IS THE MOAT AND IS NOT FOR SALE.** No fabricated fact, number, quote or attribution may ever ship. Any
80	  design that can leak one is unshippable, whatever it scores. We have a working deterministic contract
81	  (scripts/reflow_report.py): FACT (span-verified, byte-identical) / STRUCTURE (assembled from sidecar data) /
82	  INTERPRETATION (no digits, no new proper nouns, no citations, must sit beside >=2 facts from >=2 sources,
83	  contradiction-screened fail-closed). **NOTE: it was built around HEDGING — and the rubric does not reward hedging.
84	  It rewards ADJUDICATION. That contract needs rethinking, not just retuning.**
85	
86	## 6. MEASUREMENT (fixed tonight — use it, never score n=1 again)
87	- Judge noise: **SD = 0.0074** (byte-identical artifact scored 6x). Baseline is tighter (SD 0.0020).
88	- k=5 paired => SE(diff) = 0.0047 => **smallest resolvable effect = +0.0094 at 2 sigma.**
89	- Generation noise (re-composing the same config) is LARGER (~0.016) and applies ONLY to arms needing a new compose.
90	  A report->report transform of a BANKED artifact has ZERO generation noise — cheap AND sensitive. Prefer it.
91	- Our own history: baseline 0.4062 -> rank10 0.4382 = **+0.032, z=10** (real). The depth levers DID work, modestly.
92	  But every subsequent lever (padding removal, source firewall) scored FLAT. And ALL of it was aimed at proxies.
93	
94	## 7. YOUR TASK
95	Design the plan that takes POLARIS from **0.4382** to **the top of this board (0.54-0.56)**.
96	- Ground every lever in the rubric text or a measured artifact. NO PROXIES — we lost a night to them.
97	- State, for each lever: the criterion it moves, the expected points, how it FAILS, and the cheap test that proves the
98	  mechanism fired BEFORE we pay for a 65-minute compose.
99	- Respect the faithfulness moat absolutely. If a lever needs ungrounded prose, say so explicitly and justify the exact
100	  containment.
101	- Be honest about the ceiling. If your plan does not reach 0.54, SAY SO and name the residual.
102	- Where you are UNCERTAIN, SEARCH (papers, GitHub, the artifacts on disk at
103	  /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/). Do not guess.

TASK 72 PROMPT:
Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.

DIMENSION WEIGHTS: {"readability": 0.14, "insight": 0.32, "comprehensiveness": 0.29, "instruction_following": 0.25}

=== COMPREHENSIVENESS (weight 0.29) ===
- [w=0.10] Grounding in AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Assesses whether the review adequately defines AI within the context of the 4IR and explains its fundamental role as a driver of labor market restructuring. This contextual framing is crucial for understanding the scope and nature of the impacts discussed.
- [w=0.25] Breadth of Labor Market Restructuring Dimensions Covered :: Evaluates the extent to which the review addresses the multifaceted nature of AI's impact on the labor market, including job creation, job displacement, job transformation, changes in skill demands, wage effects, and productivity changes.
- [w=0.25] Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis :: Checks if the review examines the restructuring impact of AI across a diverse range of relevant industries, highlighting both common trends and sector-specific nuances. The task explicitly requires focusing on 'various industries.'
- [w=0.15] Exploration of AI's Disruptive Character and Scale :: Assesses whether the review sufficiently explores the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI, including the magnitude, speed, and transformative potential of these changes on labor market structures and dynamics.

This ensures the review's content is built upon a solid and comprehensive foundation of existing knowledge.
- [w=0.10] Balanced Discussion of AI's Labor Market Impacts :: Assesses whether the review presents a nuanced perspective by discussing both the challenges (e.g., displacement, skill gaps, inequality) and opportunities (e.g., new job creation, productivity gains, enhanced work quality) arising from AI's influence on the labor market.

- [w=0.25] Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation of AI's Disruptive Impacts Across Industries :: Evaluates the review's ability to critically synthesize diverse findings from high-quality journal articles to present a nuanced understanding of AI's 'significant disruptions.' This includes identifying patterns of impact, variations across different industries, and areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty in the literature, rather than merely cataloging effects.
- [w=0.15] Insightful Integration of AI's Role within the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Measures how effectively the review articulates and leverages the 4IR framework to provide a deeper, more contextualized understanding of the *nature, scale, and interconnectedness* of AI's transformative power on the labor market, rather than treating AI's impact in isolation or merely mentioning the 4IR superficially.
- [w=0.20] Identification and Articulation of Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives :: Assesses whether the review transcends simple summarization to identify and clearly articulate overarching emergent themes, significant theoretical linkages between concepts, or potentially novel perspectives derived from the synthesis of the reviewed literature on AI's labor market impact.
- [w=0.15] Value and Foresight in Delineating Implications and Future Research Agendas :: Evaluates the review's capacity to distill strategically valuable implications (e.g., for policy, education, workforce adaptation strategies) and to clearly delineate critical research gaps or promising future research agendas based on the comprehensive synthesis of the current state of knowledge.

def format_criteria_list(criteria_data):
    """Format evaluation criteria list as JSON string, without weight information"""
    criteria_for_prompt = {}
    criterions_dict = criteria_data.get("criterions", {})

    for dim, criterions_list in criterions_dict.items():
        if not isinstance(criterions_list, list):
            logger.warning(f"Value for dimension '{dim}' is not a list. Skipping.")
            continue

        criteria_for_prompt[dim] = []
        for crit_item in criterions_list:
            if isinstance(crit_item, dict) and "criterion" in crit_item and "explanation" in crit_item:
                criteria_for_prompt[dim].append({
                    "criterion": crit_item["criterion"],
                    "explanation": crit_item["explanation"]
                })
            else:
                logger.warning(f"Invalid criteria format in dimension '{dim}'. Skipping item.")

    try:
        return json.dumps(criteria_for_prompt, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    except TypeError as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Failed to serialize criteria to JSON: {e}")

def process_single_item(task_data, target_articles_map, reference_articles_map, criteria_map, 
                         llm_client, lock, pbar, max_retries, language):
    """Process a single task: get data, call LLM, parse results, calculate scores"""
    task_id = task_data.get('id')
    prompt = task_data.get('prompt')

    # Data retrieval and validation
    if prompt not in target_articles_map:
        logger.error(f"Target article not found for ID {task_id}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {"id": task_id, "prompt": prompt, "error": "Target article not found"}
    
    if prompt not in reference_articles_map:
        logger.error(f"Reference article not found for ID {task_id}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {"id": task_id, "prompt": prompt, "error": "Reference article not found"}
    
    if prompt not in criteria_map:
        logger.error(f"Evaluation criteria not found for ID {task_id}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {"id": task_id, "prompt": prompt, "error": "Evaluation criteria not found"}

    target_article_data = target_articles_map[prompt]
    reference_article_data = reference_articles_map[prompt]
    criteria_data = criteria_map[prompt]

    target_article = target_article_data.get('article', '')
    reference_article = reference_article_data.get('article', '')

    # Format evaluation criteria list in JSON
    try:
        criteria_list_str = format_criteria_list(criteria_data)
    except ValueError as e:
        logger.error(f"ID {task_id}: {str(e)}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {"id": task_id, "prompt": prompt, "error": f"Failed to format criteria: {str(e)}"}

    # Choose scoring prompt based on language
    merged_score_prompt = zh_merged_score_prompt if language == "zh" else en_merged_score_prompt
    
    # Prepare LLM prompt
    user_prompt = merged_score_prompt.format(
        task_prompt=prompt,
        article_1=target_article,
        article_2=reference_article,
        criteria_list=criteria_list_str 
    )

    llm_response_str = None
    llm_output_json = None
    success = False
    retry_count = 0

    while retry_count < max_retries and not success:
        try:
            llm_response_str = llm_client.generate(
                user_prompt=user_prompt,
                system_prompt=""
            )

            # Extract JSON from response
            json_str_extracted = extract_json_from_markdown(llm_response_str)
            if not json_str_extracted:
                raise ValueError("Failed to extract JSON from LLM response")
                
            llm_output_json = json.loads(json_str_extracted)
            
            # Check if all required dimensions exist
            expected_dims = ["comprehensiveness", "insight", "instruction_following", "readability"]
            if not all(dim in llm_output_json for dim in expected_dims):
                missing_dims = [dim for dim in expected_dims if dim not in llm_output_json]
                raise ValueError(f"Missing expected dimensions: {missing_dims}")
            
            # All checks passed
            success = True
            
        except Exception as e:
            retry_count += 1
            if retry_count < max_retries:
                logger.warning(f"ID {task_id}: Retry {retry_count}/{max_retries} - {str(e)}")
                time.sleep(1.5 ** retry_count)
            else:
                logger.error(f"ID {task_id}: Failed after {max_retries} retries - {str(e)}")

import logging

def calculate_weighted_scores(llm_output_json, criteria_data, language="en"):
    """
    Calculates weighted scores based on LLM output and criteria weights.
    
    Args:
        llm_output_json: JSON output from LLM with scoring data
        criteria_data: Criteria configuration with dimension weights
        language: Language of the evaluation (default: "en")
        
    Returns:
        Dictionary with weighted scores for target and reference models
    """
    results = {
        "target": {"dims": {}, "total": 0.0},
        "reference": {"dims": {}, "total": 0.0}
    }
    total_target_score = 0.0
    total_reference_score = 0.0
    
    # Get dimension weights
    dimension_weights = criteria_data.get("dimension_weight", {})
    task_id = criteria_data.get("id", "Unknown") 
    
    # Check if criterions exist
    if "criterions" not in criteria_data or not criteria_data["criterions"]:
        error_msg = f"ID: {task_id} - Missing required criterions data, cannot calculate weighted scores"
        logging.error(error_msg)
        raise ValueError(error_msg)
    
    # Create a mapping from criterion text to weight for easier lookup
    criterion_weights = {}
    for dim, criterions in criteria_data.get("criterions", {}).items():
        criterion_weights[dim] = {crit['criterion']: crit['weight'] for crit in criterions}

    # Record all unmatched criteria for warnings
    unmatched_criteria = set()
    
    for dim, scores_list in llm_output_json.items():
        if not isinstance(scores_list, list):
            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' in LLM output is not a list. Skipping.")
            continue

        if dim not in dimension_weights:
            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' from LLM output not found in criteria dimension weights. Skipping dimension.")
            continue
            
        if dim not in criterion_weights:
            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' from LLM output not found in criteria details. Skipping dimension.")
            continue

        dim_target_weighted_sum = 0.0
        dim_reference_weighted_sum = 0.0
        dim_total_weight = 0.0

        dim_criteria_map = criterion_weights.get(dim, {})
        
        # Skip dimension if no criteria mapping exists
        if not dim_criteria_map:
            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - No criteria mapping found for dimension '{dim}'. Skipping dimension.")
            continue

        for score_item in scores_list:
            if not isinstance(score_item, dict):
                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Item in scores_list for dimension '{dim}' is not a dictionary. Skipping item: {score_item}")
                continue

            criterion_text_raw = score_item.get("criterion")
            criterion_text = criterion_text_raw.strip() if isinstance(criterion_text_raw, str) else None

            # Check different score field formats
            # 1. Comparative scoring mode: article_1_score and article_2_score
            # 2. Single scoring mode: target_score
            article_1_score_raw = score_item.get("article_1_score")
            article_2_score_raw = score_item.get("article_2_score")
            target_score_raw = score_item.get("target_score")  # Single scoring mode

            # If target_score exists but article_1_score doesn't, assign target_score to article_1_score
            if target_score_raw is not None and article_1_score_raw is None:
                article_1_score_raw = target_score_raw
                # In single scoring mode, article_2_score is not needed

            try:
                article_1_score = float(article_1_score_raw) if article_1_score_raw is not None else None
                article_2_score = float(article_2_score_raw) if article_2_score_raw is not None else None
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Invalid score format for criterion '{criterion_text}' in dimension '{dim}'. Scores: art1='{article_1_score_raw}', art2='{article_2_score_raw}'. Skipping criterion.")
                continue

            # If criterion_text exists and article_1_score is not None
            if criterion_text and article_1_score is not None:
                # Check for exact match
                weight = dim_criteria_map.get(criterion_text)
                
                # If exact match not found, try fuzzy matching
                if weight is None:
                    # First try case-insensitive matching
                    criterion_lower = criterion_text.lower()
                    for key, val in dim_criteria_map.items():
                        if key.lower() == criterion_lower:
                            weight = val
                            break
                    
                    # If still not found, try substring matching
                    if weight is None:
                        for key, val in dim_criteria_map.items():
                            # Check if criterion text contains criteria or criteria contains criterion text
                            if criterion_lower in key.lower() or key.lower() in criterion_lower:
                                weight = val
                                break
                
                # If still no match found, record and use average weight
                if weight is None:
                    unmatched_criteria.add(f"{dim}:{criterion_text}")
                    # Calculate average weight for this dimension's criteria
                    weight = sum(dim_criteria_map.values()) / len(dim_criteria_map)
                    
                dim_target_weighted_sum += article_1_score * weight
                dim_total_weight += weight
                
                # Only calculate reference part if article_2_score exists
                if article_2_score is not None:
                    dim_reference_weighted_sum += article_2_score * weight
            else:
                if criterion_text:
                    if dim not in getattr(calculate_weighted_scores, '_warned_dims', set()):
                        logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Criterion text mismatch for dimension '{dim}': '{criterion_text}'. Available criteria keys start with: {list(dim_criteria_map.keys())[:1] if dim_criteria_map else []}... Check prompt/output/criteria file consistency. Further mismatches in this dim won't be logged fully.")
                        if not hasattr(calculate_weighted_scores, '_warned_dims'): calculate_weighted_scores._warned_dims = set()
                        calculate_weighted_scores._warned_dims.add(dim)
                    else:
                        logging.debug(f"ID: {task_id} - Another criterion mismatch for dimension '{dim}': '{criterion_text}'")
                elif not criterion_text:
                    logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Missing 'criterion' key in score item for dimension '{dim}': {score_item}. Skipping item.")

        if dim_total_weight > 0:
            dim_target_avg = dim_target_weighted_sum / dim_total_weight
            # Only calculate reference average if reference scores exist
            dim_reference_avg = dim_reference_weighted_sum / dim_total_weight if article_2_score is not None else 0
        else:
            dim_target_avg = 0
            dim_reference_avg = 0
            if len(scores_list) > 0:
                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - No valid criteria scored for dimension '{dim}' despite {len(scores_list)} items in LLM output. Check for systematic mismatches. Dimension average set to 0.")

        results["target"]["dims"][f"{dim}_weighted_avg"] = dim_target_avg
        results["reference"]["dims"][f"{dim}_weighted_avg"] = dim_reference_avg

        dim_weight = dimension_weights.get(dim, 0)
        total_target_score += dim_target_avg * dim_weight
        total_reference_score += dim_reference_avg * dim_weight

    # Log warning if there are unmatched criteria
    if unmatched_criteria:
        logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - {len(unmatched_criteria)} criteria without exact matches: {unmatched_criteria}")
    
    results["target"]["total"] = total_target_score
    results["reference"]["total"] = total_reference_score

    return results

print("\n=== EXACT grader sensitivity (overall = T/(T+R), T = sum_d w_d*t_d) ===")
def overall_for(X, base_overall=.4382, base_read=.3774, w=.14):
    TR = base_overall/(1-base_overall)          # T/R
    t0 = base_read/(1-base_read)                # t_read / r_read  (assume r_read ~ R)
    t1 = X/(1-X)

for X in [.40,.42,.44,.45,.452,.48,.50,.5284]:

def ceiling(wstruct, s_frac):

    num = wstruct*1.0 + (1-wstruct)*u
    Xn = num/(1+num)
    return u, Xn, overall_for(Xn)-.4382
for wstruct,label in [(.35,'S1+S2 only'),(.45,'S1+S2+F1 (generous)')]:

print("\n  For the CLAIMED readability=0.50 with w_struct=.45, solve u: needs u = 1.00  ->  our non-structural")
print("  readability must ALREADY be at FULL reference parity, and our S1/S2/F1 at 1.1/10. Not a credible band.")

print("\n=== OUR OWN 7 RUNS: does readability co-move with insight? ===")
runs={'CTRL':(.4008,.3839),'Rank7':(.3379,.4070),'Rank8':(.3634,.3903),'Rank9':(.3588,.4122),
      'Rank10':(.3774,.4238),'Rank11':(.3802,.3973),'Rank12':(.3979,.4133)}
r=[v[0] for v in runs.values()]; i=[v[1] for v in runs.values()]
n=len(r);mr=sum(r)/n;mi=sum(i)/n
cv=sum((a-mr)*(b-mi) for a,b in zip(r,i))
sr=math.sqrt(sum((a-mr)**2 for a in r)); si=math.sqrt(sum((b-mi)**2 for b in i))
print(f"  our readability across 7 runs: {sorted(r)}  SD = {math.sqrt(sum((a-mr)**2 for a in r)/n):.4f}")
print(f"  corr(readability, insight) over our own runs = {cv/(sr*si):+.2f}   <-- NO positive bleed. If anything, negative.")
print(f"  Rank9 vs Rank10 are the SAME CONFIG: readability .3588 vs .3774 = 0.0186 apart from noise alone.")

input.reasoning:
REFUTED — not on direction, but on magnitude, on mechanism, and on the specific gate it proposes. Four independent lines, all measured from the artifacts on disk and the grader source.

**1. THE BRIEF'S "H3 BAND" IS A MARKDOWN-REGEX ARTIFACT. The reference already HAS the heading tree.**
I re-parsed /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/*.jsonl counting bold-line headings, not just `###`:
| system | READ | deep (L3+) headings | med para |
|---|---|---|---|
| gemini = **THE REFERENCE** | .5100 | **34** (`**3.1. Task Automation**`) | 58w |
| bodhi | .5199 | 40 | 28w |
| WhaleCloud | .5284 | 33 | 37w |
| lunon | .5037 | 53 | 71w |
| sourcery | .5035 | 46 | 67w |
| **dalpha** | **.5284 (best on board)** | **0** | 36w |
| openai | .4859 | **0** | **161w** |
SOTA_BRIEF_V2 §4 ("the reference has 0 H3, 131w paragraphs"; "every system at/below the reference has ZERO") is false — it counted `^### ` and missed gemini's `**1.1.**` bold tree. The brief's headline structural correlate does not exist.

**2. CONTROLLING FOR SYSTEM QUALITY, THE HEADING TREE HAS THE WRONG SIGN.** Relative readability (read − overall):
- 5 systems WITH a 33-53 subsection tree: −.0242, −.0369, −.0112, −.0170, −.0002 → mean **−0.018**
- 4 systems WITHOUT: +.0032, +.0116, +.0390, +.0266 → mean **+0.020**
Perfect separation, wrong direction (exact p = 1/126). The raw corr(readability, headings)=+0.52 is 100% the "good systems are good" confound. Matched on length: dalpha (4,039w, **0** subsections) beats bodhi (4,361w, 40 subsections) on readability. openai ships **161w median / 233w p90 paragraphs with zero subsections** and its readability sits +0.012 ABOVE its own overall. Paragraph walls and missing heading trees are not what the judge punishes.

**3. THE +0.017 IS ARITHMETICALLY SELF-REFUTING.** Exact grader math (race.py: overall = T/(T+R), T=Σ w_d·t_d; score_calculator.py): +0.0094 (the resolvable floor) requires readability ≥ **0.452**; the claimed +0.017 requires readability = **0.500 = FULL PARITY WITH THE REFERENCE ON THE WHOLE DIMENSION**. But the lever only touches S1 (w=.20) + S2 (w=.15) — 35% of readability weight, 45% if you gift it F1. Solve for the rest: readability = 0.50 with w_struct = 0.45 requires u = 1.00, i.e. **our L1/P1/D1/A1 must ALREADY be at reference parity and our S1/S2/F1 at 1.1/10.** Not a credible band (the judge's floor is "4-6 Average").
Realistic ceiling, with structure driven to a straight 9.0/10 (parity — which no system on the board achieves): **+0.0076 to +0.0126 overall.** Centered ON the +0.0094 floor. This lever, executed perfectly, cannot be shown to have worked.
And the untouched 55-65% is exactly where our deficit is documented to live: **P1 (w=.15) — "rather than just serially summarizing individual papers... avoidance of redundancy"** is the literal job description of a fact conveyor, and our own logs record "one Acemoglu-Restrepo paper restated ten ways," 2,000 words of cross-section repetition (Rank11), and the judge-visible telemetry confession "No contradictions were detected by the pipeline." Splitting a serial summary into 45 paragraphs leaves it a serial summary — the lever's own ADMITTED FAILURE MODE concedes this.

**4. THE "CLAIM-FIRST H3" GATE IS FALSIFIED BY THE WINNER ARTIFACT IT CITES.** bodhi's 40 H3, measured:
- **0 / 40 would pass the lever's proposed gate.**
- 40/40 contain a **digit** (they are numbered `3.4 ...`) — the gate forbids digits.
- 40/40 contain a **proper noun** (O*NET, Germany, China, GPT) — the gate forbids new proper nouns.
- Only **9/40 (22%)** contain a finite verb. 78% are noun-phrase topic labels ("2.2 Occupational / industry / geographic exposure indices").
- The lever's own exemplar is **misquoted**. Verbatim from bodhi.jsonl: `3.4 Cross-country evidence: exposure alone does not mechanically predict job loss` — number + **topic label FIRST** + claim. The lever presents this as claim-instead-of-topic ("not 'Cross-country evidence'"). bodhi ships both, topic-first, numbered.

**ON THE ATTACK LENS — is this the failed insight directive in new clothes?** Split verdict.
- The *geometry* (split >120w, insert subsections) is genuinely different: deterministic, report→report on a banked artifact, zero generation noise, paired, cheap. That is NOT the 0.4094-vs-0.4447 experiment.
- The *claim-first heading requirement* IS. It mandates 31-50 model-synthesized argumentative claims and routes them through the WEAKEST gate in the contract: CLASS S (no digit / no citation / no proper noun / has a finite verb) — **no premise-adjacency, no contradiction screen, no empirical-outcome ban.** The lever's own exemplar, "exposure alone does not mechanically predict job loss," is a universal negative that CLASS I would reject as a bare empirical-outcome assertion in body prose — but it is made *mandatory*, 31-50×, as a heading. Headings survive ArticleCleaner (citations do not), so this puts the document's highest-salience, least-screened text in front of the judge. That is a fabrication surface, not a structural transform.

**ON BLEED (the lever's real argument, "unquantified relief on the 86%"):** zero support, wrong sign twice. Across our own 7 scored runs, corr(readability, insight) = **−0.20** (Rank12 raised readability .3774→.3979 while insight FELL .4238→.4133). Across the board, the residual analysis in (2) shows the structured systems under-perform on relative readability. "Unquantified bleed" is precisely the proxy reasoning SOTA_BRIEF_V2 §7 bans.

**NOISE:** our readability across 7 runs is [.3379 .3588 .3634 .3774 .3802 .3979 .4008], SD = **0.0207**. Rank9 and Rank10 are the SAME CONFIG and differ by 0.0186 on readability. Baselining the lever on Rank10's .3774 buys ~+0.02 of readability from regression-to-mean alone. (Mitigating: a paired report→report transform on the banked artifact cancels this — the one genuine strength here.)
input.fix:
Do not kill the geometry; kill the claim, the gate, and the bleed story. Demote L2 from a top-of-board lever to a cheap floor lever and re-point the readability budget.

1. **KEEP the reflow geometry, with a corrected target.** Split 677w walls to 40-90w and emit a subsection tree. Our −0.061 relative-readability residual is 5x worse than any system on the board and our 677w median is 4x the worst other (openai, 161w) — that outlier is worth repairing. But budget it at **+0.006 to +0.013**, not +0.017, and state it as a floor lever.

2. **DELETE the claim-first heading check.** It rejects 40/40 of the winner's own headings and describes only 22% of them. Replace it with the winner's ACTUAL measured heading grammar: `<number> <topic-label noun phrase>[: <claim>]`, numbered, proper nouns permitted, ~25-30% carrying a post-colon claim. Derive headings EXTRACTIVELY from the section's dominant content stems — that keeps them inside CLASS S with no new claim surface at all. If any claim-bearing heading survives, it must pass the FULL CLASS I screen (premise adjacency + contradiction screen + empirical-outcome ban), not the four-regex CLASS S gate; a heading is the single most judge-salient string in the document and currently the only judge-visible text with no faithfulness screen (ArticleCleaner strips citations, never headings).

3. **RE-POINT the readability effort at the 55-65% of the dimension the lever does not touch — which is where our deficit is documented.** P1 (w=.15, "rather than just serially summarizing individual papers... avoidance of redundancy") and L1 (w=.20, academic tone) are the criteria a fact conveyor structurally fails. Concrete, already-evidenced targets: delete the "No contradictions were detected by the pipeline" telemetry confession sitting inside the critical-synthesis section; kill the restatement pattern (one Acemoglu-Restrepo paper rendered ten ways). These cost nothing and hit weight the reflow cannot reach.

4. **PRE-REGISTER THE KILL BAR BEFORE SPENDING.** Because the transform is report→report on the banked Rank10 artifact, run k=5 PAIRED (zero generation noise, judge noise only). Pass bar, derived exactly from the grader: **readability_norm ≥ 0.452** — that is the precise threshold at which this lever clears the +0.0094 resolvable floor. Below 0.452, kill it; do not let "structure obviously helps" or "bleed" rescue it. Also pre-register the falsifier for bleed: if readability rises and insight does not move, the 86% story is dead (as our own 7-run corr = −0.20 already predicts).
